meaning: n.<美俚>(政府首脑向劳工界领袖发出的限制价格,工资等的)施工呼吁
examples: In the past jawboning had some effect.
The debate included a lot of jawboning as each candidate tried to persuade voters to see the issues his way.
To provide additional monetary stimulus, central Banks can only turn to negative rates, to quantitative easing, or to jawboning of markets.
All we got out of this market and greenback jawboning was a massive stock-market correction.
Here in America vast amounts of stimulus money and White House jawboning have done little to move the unemployment needle, frozen at 9.5%.
